title: CholecT50 reasoning - clinician review
intro_md:
Watch each clip, then rate the model's **answer** and its three-level **reasoning** `<think>`.

The reasoning is always written in three labelled levels (**L1 -> L2 -> L3**); what each level should contain depends on the task. A reminder of the levels **for the current task** stays pinned under the video on every item, so you can rate against it as you read.
intro_md_zh:
观看每个片段，然后评价模型的**回答**及其三层**推理** `<think>`。

推理始终按三个带标签的层级书写（**L1 -> L2 -> L3**）；每个层级应包含的内容取决于任务。**当前任务**对应的层级说明会固定显示在每个条目的视频下方，便于您边阅读边评价。

TAL.q: when does a given phase happen?

levels.label: phase signature & onset cue
levels.desc: Names the queried phase and its visual hallmark - the specific instruments, actions and anatomy that define it - and pinpoints the moment it first becomes visible (its onset), grounded in what is on screen rather than a guess.

levels.label: boundary contrast
levels.desc: Distinguishes the phase from the activity immediately before and after it, using the change in instruments/actions to fix where the phase ends (its offset) - so both edges of the window are justified by observation, not just the middle.

levels.label: phase-order bound & conclusion
levels.desc: Places the phase within the fixed cholecystectomy phase order to confirm the window is consistent with the workflow, then states the final time span as the conclusion.

levels.label: current step at the clip's end
levels.desc: From the activity in the FINAL part of the clip (not the early-clip activity), identifies the phase currently in progress by its instruments, action and structures.

levels.label: completion cue
levels.desc: Gives the observed evidence that the current phase's objective is essentially achieved (target divided / freed / packed / fossa cleared), so that a transition to the next phase is due.

levels.label: procedural logic & conclusion
levels.desc: Applies the fixed phase order - the step following the current one is the next action - to name the predicted next phase, or justifies a non-textbook successor from the observed state (e.g. residual bleeding -> coagulation revisited).

levels.label: scan & enumerate
levels.desc: Sweeping the clip from start to end, lists which candidate phases appear and in what order, by their visual signatures; phases that never appear are omitted.

levels.label: boundaries
levels.desc: For each adjacent pair of segments, states the transition cue (the change in instrument/action) that fixes the boundary, so the segments are contiguous and non-overlapping.

levels.label: per-segment content & conclusion
levels.desc: For each segment in order, gives the key instrument-action-structure its caption must carry, then concludes with the list of [start, end] segments and their headline captions.

levels.label: survey the arc
levels.desc: Surveys the clip from start to end, naming the phases/activities it moves through and the overall direction of the procedure.

levels.label: salience & transitions
levels.desc: Separates the salient through-line (the defining actions) from minor or repeated maneuvers that can be compressed, and marks where the main transitions occur.

levels.label: compress & conclusion
levels.desc: Compresses the arc into the high-level summary the final answer states - the condensed account of what the surgeon accomplishes across the clip.

do: Keep exactly three levels (L1 -> L2 -> L3), in order; ground every statement in observed evidence (instruments, actions, tissue/structures, timing).

Use precise vocabulary: instruments (grasper, hook, scissors, clipper, bipolar, irrigator), the seven phases, the Calot (hepatocystic) triangle, anatomy (cystic duct, cystic artery, gallbladder, liver bed/fossa).
Write as if you are watching the video; make L3 conclude the answer; be concise.
Justify the span from the onset/offset activity; do NOT merely restate the time.
Timestamps ARE allowed (the span is the answer).
dont: Do not reveal the facts were provided: never write "given", "as provided", "the label", "the answer", "ground truth", and do not refer to "the captions/observations/text".
Do not invent anything not observed (no patient history, no off-screen events, no measurements).
Do not skip, merge, reorder, or add a level.
Do not hedge or list alternatives ("possibly X or Y") — commit to the reasoning that reaches the answer.
Do not invent a span for an absent phase — conclude it does not occur.

do: Keep exactly three levels (L1 -> L2 -> L3), in order; ground every statement in observed evidence (instruments, actions, tissue/structures, timing).

Use precise vocabulary: instruments (grasper, hook, scissors, clipper, bipolar, irrigator), the seven phases, the Calot (hepatocystic) triangle, anatomy (cystic duct, cystic artery, gallbladder, liver bed/fossa).
Write as if you are watching the video; make L3 conclude the answer; be concise.
Read the END of the clip to set the current phase.
dont: Do not reveal the facts were provided: never write "given", "as provided", "the label", "the answer", "ground truth", and do not refer to "the captions/observations/text".
Do not invent anything not observed (no patient history, no off-screen events, no measurements).
Do not skip, merge, reorder, or add a level.
Do not hedge or list alternatives ("possibly X or Y") — commit to the reasoning that reaches the answer.
Do not base the current phase on early-clip activity.

do: Keep exactly three levels (L1 -> L2 -> L3), in order; ground every statement in observed evidence (instruments, actions, tissue/structures, timing).

Use precise vocabulary: instruments (grasper, hook, scissors, clipper, bipolar, irrigator), the seven phases, the Calot (hepatocystic) triangle, anatomy (cystic duct, cystic artery, gallbladder, liver bed/fossa).
Write as if you are watching the video; make L3 conclude the answer; be concise.
Keep L3 at HEADLINE level (one short phrase per segment).
Justify non-overlap/contiguity at L2.
Timestamps ARE allowed.
dont: Do not reveal the facts were provided: never write "given", "as provided", "the label", "the answer", "ground truth", and do not refer to "the captions/observations/text".
Do not invent anything not observed (no patient history, no off-screen events, no measurements).
Do not skip, merge, reorder, or add a level.
Do not hedge or list alternatives ("possibly X or Y") — commit to the reasoning that reaches the answer.
Do NOT write the full captions inside the think — that duplicates the answer; headline only.
Do not include phases that are absent (it is a filter).

Use precise vocabulary: instruments (grasper, hook, scissors, clipper, bipolar, irrigator), the seven phases, the Calot (hepatocystic) triangle, anatomy (cystic duct, cystic artery, gallbladder, liver bed/fossa).
Write as if you are watching the video; make L3 conclude the answer; be concise.
Select and compress (arc + salience).
Lowercase instrument names; observable steps only.
dont: Do not reveal the facts were provided: never write "given", "as provided", "the label", "the answer", "ground truth", and do not refer to "the captions/observations/text".
Do not invent anything not observed (no patient history, no off-screen events, no measurements).
Do not skip, merge, reorder, or add a level.
Do not hedge or list alternatives ("possibly X or Y") — commit to the reasoning that reaches the answer.
Do NOT enumerate per segment or include timestamps (that is DVC, not VS).
Do not use 'indicates' / 'suggesting that' phrasing.
Do not restate the whole summary inside L1/L2.

TAL.context:
**Temporal Action Localization (TAL).** The model watched this clip and was asked *when a specific surgical phase occurs*. It had to answer with a time span (start-end, in seconds) and justify that span with a three-level reasoning trace. You are validating two things: whether the **answer (the time span) is correct**, and whether the **reasoning genuinely supports it** from what is visible in the video.

**What you can edit:** the model's reasoning and the prompt's DO/DON'T rules are editable here (the time-span answer is read-only for this task). Every edit you make is saved with your response.
TAL.context_zh:
**时间动作定位（TAL）。** 模型观看了该片段，并被要求判断*某个特定手术阶段在何时发生*。它需要给出一个时间区间（起止，以秒为单位），并用三层推理加以论证。您需要验证两点：**答案（时间区间）是否正确**，以及**推理是否真正依据视频中可见的内容支持该答案**。

**可编辑内容：** 此处可编辑模型的推理以及提示词的应当/不应当规则（本任务的时间区间答案为只读）。您的所有编辑都会随评审结果一并保存。
steps: Play the clip and scrub to the highlighted window and its start/end edges.
Read the question (which phase is asked about) and the model's answer (the time span).
Decide whether the answer's start and end match what you actually see.
Read the model's reasoning and check each level against the pinned guide - L1 visible onset cue, L2 boundary contrast, L3 phase-order check.
Open the collapsible 'Prompt rules' panel and check the reasoning obeys the DO / DON'T.
Rate every question below (faithfulness, logic, L1/L2/L3 correctness, overall, main error), and add a comment under any rating where it helps.
Edit the model's reasoning directly in its box to fix anything wrong (use 'Reset to original' to undo). The time-span answer is read-only for this task - note any answer error in a comment.
You can also edit the DO / DON'T text in the 'Prompt rules' panel. Every edit is saved with your response.
If you cannot assess this clip, click 'Skip this item'.
steps_zh: 播放片段，并拖动到高亮区间及其起止边界处。
阅读问题（询问的是哪个阶段）和模型的答案（时间区间）。
判断答案的起点和终点是否与您实际看到的相符。
阅读模型的推理，并对照固定显示的层级说明逐层检查——L1 可见的起始线索，L2 边界对比，L3 阶段顺序确认。
展开可折叠的「提示词规则」面板，检查推理是否遵守应当/不应当。
对下方每个问题评分（忠实度、逻辑、L1/L2/L3 是否正确、总体、主要错误），并在需要时在任意评分下添加备注。
直接在文本框中编辑模型的推理以更正任何错误（点击「恢复原文」可撤销）。本任务的时间区间答案为只读——如有答案错误请在备注中说明。
您也可以在「提示词规则」面板中编辑应当/不应当文本。所有编辑都会随评审结果一并保存。
若您无法评估该片段，请点击「跳过该条目」。

NAP.context:
**Next Action Prediction (NAP).** The model watched this clip and was asked to *predict the next surgical phase* that follows after the clip ends. It had to name that next phase and justify it with a three-level reasoning trace. You are validating whether the **predicted next phase is correct**, and whether the **reasoning reads the END of the clip correctly** to get there.

**What you can edit:** the model's reasoning and the prompt's DO/DON'T rules are editable here (the predicted-phase answer is read-only for this task). Every edit you make is saved with your response.
NAP.context_zh:
**下一步动作预测（NAP）。** 模型观看了该片段，并被要求*预测片段结束后接下来的手术阶段*。它需要指出下一阶段，并用三层推理加以论证。您需要验证**预测的下一阶段是否正确**，以及**推理是否正确解读了片段的结尾**来得出该结论。

**可编辑内容：** 此处可编辑模型的推理以及提示词的应当/不应当规则（本任务的预测阶段答案为只读）。您的所有编辑都会随评审结果一并保存。
steps: Play the clip, paying close attention to the FINAL seconds (the phase in progress at the end).
Read the question and the model's predicted next phase (the answer).
Decide whether that is the correct next phase given the clip's ending state.
Read the model's reasoning - L1 phase in progress at the clip's end, L2 evidence its objective is essentially complete, L3 procedural-order logic to the next phase.
Open the collapsible 'Prompt rules' panel and check the reasoning obeys the DO / DON'T (the current phase must be read from the END, not early-clip activity).
Rate every question below, and add a comment under any rating where it helps.
Edit the model's reasoning directly in its box to fix anything wrong (use 'Reset to original' to undo). The predicted-phase answer is read-only for this task - note any answer error in a comment.
You can also edit the DO / DON'T text in the 'Prompt rules' panel. Every edit is saved with your response.
If you cannot assess this clip, click 'Skip this item'.
steps_zh: 播放片段，特别留意最后几秒（结尾处正在进行的阶段）。
阅读问题和模型预测的下一阶段（答案）。
根据片段结尾的状态，判断这是否为正确的下一阶段。
阅读模型的推理——L1 片段结尾正在进行的阶段，L2 该阶段目标基本完成的证据，L3 通向下一阶段的流程顺序逻辑。
展开可折叠的「提示词规则」面板，检查推理是否遵守应当/不应当（当前阶段必须依据结尾判断，而非片段早期的操作）。
对下方每个问题评分，并在需要时在任意评分下添加备注。
直接在文本框中编辑模型的推理以更正任何错误（点击「恢复原文」可撤销）。本任务的预测阶段答案为只读——如有答案错误请在备注中说明。
您也可以在「提示词规则」面板中编辑应当/不应当文本。所有编辑都会随评审结果一并保存。
若您无法评估该片段，请点击「跳过该条目」。

DVC.context:
**Dense Video Captioning (DVC).** The model watched this clip and was asked to *break it into phase segments* - for each segment give a [start, end] time and a short description - then justify the segmentation with a three-level reasoning trace. You are validating whether the **segments, their boundaries, and their descriptions are correct**, and whether the reasoning supports them.

**What you can edit:** the model's answer (the segment list), its reasoning, and the prompt's DO/DON'T rules are all editable here. Every edit you make is saved with your response.
DVC.context_zh:
**密集视频描述（DVC）。** 模型观看了该片段，并被要求*将其划分为多个阶段时间段*——为每段给出 [起始, 结束] 时间和简短描述——并用三层推理论证该划分。您需要验证**各时间段、其边界及描述是否正确**，以及推理是否支持它们。

**可编辑内容：** 此处可编辑模型的答案（时间段列表）、推理以及提示词的应当/不应当规则。您的所有编辑都会随评审结果一并保存。
steps: Play the clip and note which phases appear and roughly when they change.
Read the model's answer - the list of segments with their times and descriptions.
Check the segments are correct, contiguous and non-overlapping, with none invented or missing.
Read the model's reasoning - L1 enumerates the phases in order, L2 justifies each boundary by a visible transition cue, L3 gives a one-line headline per segment.
Open the collapsible 'Prompt rules' panel and check the reasoning obeys the DO / DON'T.
Rate every question below, and add a comment under any rating where it helps.
Edit the model's ANSWER (segment list, times, descriptions) AND its reasoning directly in their boxes to fix anything wrong (use 'Reset to original' to undo).
You can also edit the DO / DON'T text in the 'Prompt rules' panel. Every edit is saved with your response.
If you cannot assess this clip, click 'Skip this item'.
steps_zh: 播放片段，留意出现了哪些阶段以及它们大致何时切换。
阅读模型的答案——包含各时间段及其时间和描述的列表。
检查各时间段是否正确、连续且不重叠，没有臆造或遗漏。
阅读模型的推理——L1 按顺序枚举各阶段，L2 通过可见的转换线索论证每个边界，L3 为每段给出一句标题。
展开可折叠的「提示词规则」面板，检查推理是否遵守应当/不应当。
对下方每个问题评分，并在需要时在任意评分下添加备注。
直接在文本框中编辑模型的【答案】（时间段列表、时间、描述）以及其推理以更正任何错误（点击「恢复原文」可撤销）。
您也可以在「提示词规则」面板中编辑应当/不应当文本。所有编辑都会随评审结果一并保存。
若您无法评估该片段，请点击「跳过该条目」。

VS.context:
**Video Summarization (VS).** The model watched this clip and was asked to *summarize the whole procedure* in a short paragraph (no per-segment timestamps), then justify it with a three-level reasoning trace. You are validating whether the **summary is faithful, complete and well-formed**, and whether the reasoning supports it.

**What you can edit:** the model's answer (the summary), its reasoning, and the prompt's DO/DON'T rules are all editable here. Every edit you make is saved with your response.
VS.context_zh:
**视频总结（VS）。** 模型观看了该片段，并被要求用一段简短文字*总结整个手术过程*（不含逐段时间戳），并用三层推理加以论证。您需要验证**总结是否忠实、完整、表述恰当**，以及推理是否支持它。

**可编辑内容：** 此处可编辑模型的答案（总结）、推理以及提示词的应当/不应当规则。您的所有编辑都会随评审结果一并保存。
steps: Play the whole clip to get its overall arc.
Read the model's summary (the answer).
Check it is factually correct, covers the main surgical activities, omits nothing important, and invents nothing.
Read the model's reasoning - L1 surveys the arc, L2 separates the salient actions from minor/repeated ones, L3 compresses to the final summary.
Open the collapsible 'Prompt rules' panel and check the reasoning obeys the DO / DON'T.
Rate every question below, and add a comment under any rating where it helps.
Edit the model's SUMMARY (the answer) AND its reasoning directly in their boxes - rewrite anything wrong (use 'Reset to original' to undo).
You can also edit the DO / DON'T text in the 'Prompt rules' panel. Every edit is saved with your response.
If you cannot assess this clip, click 'Skip this item'.
steps_zh: 完整播放片段，了解其整体过程。
阅读模型的总结（答案）。
检查其是否事实正确、涵盖主要手术操作、无重要遗漏、且无臆造。
阅读模型的推理——L1 梳理整体过程，L2 将显著操作与次要/重复操作区分开，L3 压缩为最终总结。
展开可折叠的「提示词规则」面板，检查推理是否遵守应当/不应当。
对下方每个问题评分，并在需要时在任意评分下添加备注。
直接在文本框中编辑模型的【总结】（答案）以及其推理——重写任何有误之处（点击「恢复原文」可撤销）。
您也可以在「提示词规则」面板中编辑应当/不应当文本。所有编辑都会随评审结果一并保存。
若您无法评估该片段，请点击「跳过该条目」。

dimensions.label: Reasoning <think> is faithful to the video (no invented detail)
L1 is correct (see the pinned levels for this task)
L3 is correct, and the final conclusion follows from it

items.question: You are an expert surgical analyst specializing in minimally invasive procedures. This video shows an endoscopic view of a laparoscopic cholecystectomy, a keyhole surgery to remove the gallbladder. The video may contain the following phases: preparation, carlot-triangle-dissection, clipping-and-cutting, gallbladder-dissection, gallbladder-packaging, cleaning-and-coagulation, gallbladder-extraction. When does gallbladder-packaging happen?
items.think:
L1 (phase signature & onset): Gallbladder-packaging is identified by graspers unfolding an endoscopic specimen_bag on the liver surface, opening the translucent pouch, placing the detached gallbladder inside, and cinching the drawstring; this becomes visible at the start of the clip around 0.0 seconds.

L2 (boundary contrast): No preceding operative phase is visible before this packaging activity, while the endpoint is fixed by the shift after the bag is closed and drawn toward the trocar: at about 98.0 seconds the graspers instead retract the liver and work on the cauterized gallbladder fossa with cleaning/coagulation contact.

L3 (phase-order bound & conclusion): In the sequence preparation -> Calot-triangle-dissection -> clipping-and-cutting -> gallbladder-dissection -> gallbladder-packaging -> cleaning-and-coagulation -> gallbladder-extraction, the bagging of the detached gallbladder immediately before liver-bed cleaning confirms gallbladder-packaging spans 0.0-97.0 seconds.

items.question: You are an expert surgical video reviewer. This endoscopic footage captures the steps of a laparoscopic cholecystectomy, a minimally invasive gallbladder removal surgery. The video may contain the following phases: preparation, carlot-triangle-dissection, clipping-and-cutting, gallbladder-dissection, gallbladder-packaging, cleaning-and-coagulation, gallbladder-extraction. When does gallbladder-packaging happen?
items.think:
L1 (phase signature & onset): Gallbladder-packaging is identified by graspers unfolding a translucent specimen_bag, placing the detached gallbladder into it, and cinching the bag closed; this is visible from the start of the clip at 0.0 seconds.

L2 (boundary contrast): There is no preceding operative phase visible before the bag handling begins, while the activity changes after 97.0 seconds to liver retraction, exposure of the cauterized gallbladder fossa, and cleaning/coagulation of the liver bed rather than packing the gallbladder.

L3 (phase-order bound & conclusion): In the fixed sequence, gallbladder-packaging follows gallbladder-dissection and precedes cleaning-and-coagulation, matching the transition from bagging the detached gallbladder to treating the liver bed; therefore gallbladder-packaging spans 0.0–97.0 seconds.

items.question: You are a specialist in analyzing minimally invasive surgery videos. This recording shows a laparoscopic cholecystectomy, a procedure to remove the gallbladder through small abdominal incisions. Considering these surgical phases: ['preparation', 'carlot-triangle-dissection', 'clipping-and-cutting', 'gallbladder-dissection', 'gallbladder-packaging', 'cleaning-and-coagulation', 'gallbladder-extraction'], what is the most likely next phase?
items.think:
L1: At the clip’s end, the current phase is cleaning-and-coagulation: graspers retract/elevate the liver while the instrument tip works along the cauterized gallbladder fossa and adjacent clipped tissue to clear/coagulate the liver bed.

L2: The gallbladder is already detached and packed/cinched inside the specimen_bag, and the exposed liver bed has been repeatedly inspected and treated, with the instruments shifting from the fossa back toward the bag.

L3: In the fixed laparoscopic cholecystectomy sequence, once cleaning-and-coagulation has essentially cleared the fossa after the gallbladder is bagged, the next procedural action is removal of the bagged specimen; therefore the next step is gallbladder-extraction.

items.question: You are an expert in surgical video interpretation. The following clip depicts a laparoscopic cholecystectomy, a minimally invasive operation for gallbladder removal, captured via an endoscopic camera. From the list of possible phases: ['preparation', 'carlot-triangle-dissection', 'clipping-and-cutting', 'gallbladder-dissection', 'gallbladder-packaging', 'cleaning-and-coagulation', 'gallbladder-extraction'], which one should follow the current segment?
items.think:
L1: At the clip’s end, the grasper retracts/elevates the liver and works along the moist, bloody, cauterized gallbladder fossa, contacting the liver bed and clipped tissue for cleaning-and-coagulation.

L2: The gallbladder is already detached and packed in the cinched specimen_bag, and the liver bed has been exposed and treated; the instrument then moves away from the fossa toward the specimen_bag, indicating the cleaning-and-coagulation objective is essentially complete.

L3: In laparoscopic cholecystectomy, after cleaning-and-coagulation follows removal of the bagged specimen through the trocar site, so the next step is gallbladder-extraction.

items.question: You are an expert surgical video reviewer. This endoscopic footage captures the steps of a laparoscopic cholecystectomy, a minimally invasive gallbladder removal surgery. The procedure includes the following phases: ['preparation', 'carlot-triangle-dissection', 'clipping-and-cutting', 'gallbladder-dissection', 'gallbladder-packaging', 'cleaning-and-coagulation', 'gallbladder-extraction']. What comes next after the current video?
items.think:
L1: At the end of the clip, the procedure is in Calot/hepatocystic triangle dissection: graspers retract the gallbladder neck/infundibulum while a hook/white-tipped dissector, bipolar, and irrigator work around the cystic duct region and gallbladder–liver interface.

L2: The dissection plane has been widened, bleeding points have been controlled with bipolar coagulation, bloody fluid has been cleared, and the cystic duct region/gray tubular structure is exposed under sustained traction, indicating the Calot dissection objective is essentially achieved.

L3: In the fixed laparoscopic cholecystectomy sequence, once Calot triangle dissection has exposed the ductal structures sufficiently, the next phase is clipping-and-cutting of the cystic duct/artery structures; therefore the next step is clipping-and-cutting.

items.question: You are an expert surgical video reviewer. This endoscopic footage captures the steps of a laparoscopic cholecystectomy, a minimally invasive gallbladder removal surgery. Considering these surgical phases: ['preparation', 'carlot-triangle-dissection', 'clipping-and-cutting', 'gallbladder-dissection', 'gallbladder-packaging', 'cleaning-and-coagulation', 'gallbladder-extraction'], what is the most likely next phase?
items.think:
L1 (current step at the clip's END): The final activity is gallbladder-packaging: graspers spread the specimen_bag on the liver surface, place the detached gallbladder into the translucent pouch, and cinch/draw the bag toward the trocar while retracting the liver near the liver bed/fossa.

L2 (completion cue): The gallbladder is already freed from the liver bed and packed inside the partially collapsed specimen_bag, with the adjacent dissected fossa exposed rather than undergoing further hook dissection or Calot triangle work.

L3 (procedural logic & conclusion): After gallbladder-packaging, the operative sequence proceeds to inspection, suction/irrigation, and hemostasis of the exposed liver bed; therefore the next step is cleaning-and-coagulation.

items.question: You are an expert in operative video analysis. The following video contains endoscopic footage of a laparoscopic cholecystectomy, where the gallbladder is removed using small incisions and specialized instruments. items.answer:

items.question: You are a specialist in analyzing minimally invasive surgery videos. For each event: (1) start_time, (2) end_time, (3) description of this event.
items.answer:
0.0-20.0 seconds: During the preparation phase, a grasper first secures the gallbladder near the fundus and retracts it superiorly against the liver, after which a second grasper enters from the left to grasp the lower gallbladder/infundibular region and maintain lateral traction while a white-tipped instrument briefly approaches the liver surface without visible dissection.

items.question: You are an expert in operative video analysis. The following video contains endoscopic footage of a laparoscopic cholecystectomy, where the gallbladder is removed using small incisions and specialized instruments. Describe the overall surgical procedure shown in the video without listing specific time segments.
items.answer: A grasper secured the gallbladder fundus and retracted it superiorly, while a second grasper held the infundibular region laterally to set exposure. The hook/white-tipped dissector opened fibrofatty peritoneal tissue around the Calot/hepatocystic triangle and gallbladder–liver interface, enlarging a pale-to-bloody dissection plane. bipolar coagulation controlled bleeding points, and irrigation/aspiration cleared bloody fluid from the field. Continued traction exposed the cystic duct region, a gray tubular structure, and inflamed liver-bed attachments for further separation.
items.think:
L1 (survey the arc): The clip begins in the preparation phase with a grasper elevating the gallbladder fundus against the liver while a second grasper takes the infundibulum/lower gallbladder laterally for exposure. It then proceeds into Calot (hepatocystic) triangle dissection, where the hook/white-tipped dissector works along the cystic duct region and gallbladder–liver bed interface, followed by bipolar hemostasis and irrigation/aspiration.

L2 (salience & transitions): The defining transition is from simple exposure to active opening of fibrofatty peritoneal tissue around the hepatocystic triangle, with traction repeatedly adjusted to keep the infundibulum away from the liver and widen the working plane. Recurrent regrasping and minor instrument approaches are compressed, while the salient events are formation of a pale-to-bloody dissection plane, control of bleeding points with bipolar, clearing of bloody fluid with the irrigator, and exposure of a gray tubular cystic duct-region structure.

L3 (compress & conclusion): Overall, the sequence shows gallbladder retraction to establish exposure, then Calot triangle and liver-bed dissection with hook/white-tipped instrumentation, hemostasis, suction/irrigation, and continued traction to expose the cystic duct region and inflamed attachments for further separation.

items.question: You are an expert surgical analyst specializing in minimally invasive procedures. This video shows an endoscopic view of a laparoscopic cholecystectomy, a keyhole surgery to remove the gallbladder. Describe the overall surgical procedure shown in the video without listing specific time segments.
items.answer: A grasper retracts the gallbladder fundus superiorly against the liver while another grasper holds the infundibulum laterally to set tension. A hook/white-tipped dissector opens fibrofatty peritoneal tissue around the cystic duct region and along the gallbladder–liver interface, widening the dissection plane with intermittent minor bleeding. bipolar coagulates visible vessels and bleeding points, and the irrigator clears bloody fluid. Continued traction exposes the cystic duct region, a gray tubular structure, and inflamed liver-bed attachments for further separation.
items.think:
L1: The clip begins in the preparation phase with a grasper lifting the gallbladder fundus superiorly against the liver and a second grasper taking the infundibulum for lateral traction. It then proceeds into Calot/hepatocystic triangle dissection, where a hook/white-tipped dissector works around the cystic duct region and gallbladder–liver interface.

L2: The defining actions are sustained fundus and infundibular retraction, opening of fibrofatty peritoneal tissue, widening of the dissection plane, bipolar control of small bleeding points, and irrigator clearance of bloody fluid. Repeated traction adjustments and minor instrument repositioning are compressed because they serve the same goal of exposing the cystic duct region and liver-bed attachments.

L3: The high-level progression is from exposure and tensioning of the gallbladder to active Calot triangle and liver-bed dissection with hemostasis and irrigation. The scene summarizes preparation followed by hepatocystic triangle dissection: graspers maintain traction while a hook/white-tipped dissector separates fibrofatty tissue, bipolar coagulates visible bleeding, and the irrigator clears the field as the cystic duct region, gray tubular structure, and inflamed liver-bed attachments become exposed.
